remember: Recall the definition of definite integral as a limit of sum.
Recall the formula for definite integral of a function f(x) from a to b.
Recall the properties of definite integrals such as additivity, linearity, etc.
understand: Understand the concept of indefinite integral and its relationship with the definite integral.
Understand the concept of the antiderivative and how it is used to evaluate definite integrals.
Understand the geometric interpretation of definite integrals as the area under curves.
apply: Apply the formula for definite integral to evaluate integrals of various functions.
Apply the properties of definite integrals to simplify and evaluate integrals.
Use the concept of antiderivative to evaluate definite integrals.
analyze: Analyze the physical significance of definite integrals in various real-world problems.
Analyze the relationship between definite integrals and other mathematical concepts such as derivatives.
Analyze the geometric interpretation of definite integrals and its applications.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of definite integral as a limit of sum.
To learn the properties of definite integrals and their applications.
To understand the concept of antiderivative and its relationship with definite integrals.
board_exam_objectives: Evaluate definite integrals using the formula and properties.
Apply the concept of antiderivative to evaluate definite integrals.
Understand the geometric interpretation of definite integrals and its applications.
